scenario: Cross-project intake confirms existing guidance and stays no-op
contextMode: saved report freshness check plus fresh temporary scans
fixtureState: Hazakura Habitat SwiftPM checkout with read-only sibling-project intake; generated report output and local paths are omitted.
taskSummary: Run the daily Habitat development loop, refresh stale or missing sibling-project reports into temporary output, and decide whether the external intake justifies a Habitat-side change.
relevantHabitatSignals: Local Habitat report still selected SwiftPM validation.
The Android saved report was stale after project guidance changed.
Fresh Android scan selected the existing Gradle wrapper and documented project-local validation script guidance.
The Python ledger project had no saved report, and a fresh scan selected the project virtual environment.
Existing behavior fixtures already covered the stale-report, project-local validation script, and ledger freshness boundaries.

prefer: refresh stale sibling reports into temporary output
continue the local Habitat slice when external intake confirms current behavior
doNot: copy raw report output into Nenrin
add Android or Python workflow scope without a new command-decision gap
reviewFirst: Open command_policy.md before risky local mutation, not for read-only temporary scans.

withSavedReportOnly.firstProposedAction: Treat the Android report as bounded-stale because key guidance changed after the saved scan.
commandsProposed: compare saved report timestamp with visible project guidance mtimes
commandsActuallyRun: compare saved report timestamp with visible project guidance mtimes
withSavedReportOnly.risk: Trusting saved or missing reports would make external intake look quieter than the current project facts justify.

withHabitatContext.firstProposedAction: Refresh stale or missing sibling reports into temporary output, then treat matching guidance as no-op intake instead of expanding Habitat scope.
commandsProposed: record no new Habitat carry-back when fresh guidance matches existing behavior fixtures
avoidedCommands: copy raw report output into Nenrin
create duplicate behavior fixtures for already-covered signals

evidenceNormalizationImplication: No new normalized evidence shape is justified. The useful command decision is the no-op boundary: fresh external scans can confirm current Habitat behavior, and that should stop external intake from becoming a second workstream.

verdict.reason: The run refreshed stale or missing sibling-project context into temporary output, confirmed current Gradle wrapper, project-local script, and Python virtualenv guidance, and avoided duplicate Habitat changes or watched-project edits.
verdict.followUpImprovement: Only add another external-intake fixture if a fresh scan changes command guidance beyond the existing stale-report, wrapper-script, ledger, or blocker cases.

artifactImprovementIfFailed: If future runs keep manufacturing Habitat changes after external intake confirms current behavior, tighten automation wording around clean no-op intake before adding scanner features.
